rows.notes: Top-of-page title. Ink is clear and centered; no monetary columns present. Spelling transcribed as it appears ('Recepte Anni presentis').
Second header line beneath title. Letters slightly stylistic but readable. No amounts; appears as a section heading.

Description legible as 'Guinear.' Amount cluster at top-right (10 | 13 | 11) appears aligned to this first entry. Alignment to columns is moderately uncertain because these top-right numerals sit close to header area; numbers themselves are reasonably clear but placement could belong to an adjacent line.
Place name is fairly clear as 'Longwilson.' No amount visible on the £/s/d columns aligned to this row.
Description reads as 'Pinkhurst pro Annunce' (old Latin 'pro Annuo/Annue' style). Amount '12 0 0' aligned clearly to this row; column boundaries evident and values are in valid ranges.
Place name is legible as a South- name; read as 'Southnewington' though middle letters are slightly compressed and could be 'Southunnington' or similar. No amount visible for this row.
Reads 'Eddington Pri' (the final letters look like 'Pri' or 'Pri.' — possibly abbreviation). No monetary figures aligned to this line.
Clear place name 'Garrington.' No amount in the £/s/d columns for this row.
Clear single-word entry 'Norton.' No monetary values present.
Read as 'Chace Hill' (spelling possibly 'Chase Hill'). No amount on the right columns.
Short name read as 'Shrup.' Handwriting slightly cramped; could be 'Shrewp'/'Shrub' but recorded as it appears. No amount visible.
Description 'Eddington vicar' is clear. Amount '4 0 0' is well aligned in the three narrow right-hand columns; digits crisp and within valid ranges.
Longer description 'Lucas Eaton by Mr Broadwater' legible. Amount '2 0 0' clearly aligned in the £/s/d columns; digits unambiguous.
Description read as 'Pre quite Routh Annu' (old-style abbreviation; letters clear enough). Amount '2 13 7' is crisply written and well aligned. All currency columns validate (13 shillings, 7 pence).
Single-word entry 'Warner.' No amount visible in the £/s/d columns.

Reads approximately 'Ralph Marshalls Capor.' The surname and final word are somewhat abbreviated/ink-blotted; no monetary figures aligned with this line. Dot beneath the pound column (single ink spot) but not a numeric value.
Description 'Lord Annu.' (abbreviated) is legible. Amount '2 1 2' is visible; alignment to columns is clear and numbers are within valid ranges.
Place name 'Calcot.' clear. No amount present in the monetary columns.
Reads 'Cuddington Mill' (final word 'Mill' readable). No monetary figures aligned to this row.
Single-word entry 'Bampton' clear and inked; no amount present.
Read as 'Mountacute.' (old place name spelling). No monetary figures on the right columns.
Short name interpreted as 'Hughop' though letters are compressed and could be 'Hugh??'. No amount present.
Clear 'Greenway.' entry. No monetary values next to it.
Entry reads 'Baufter' (handwriting suggests 'Bausfer'/'Baufter'); a horizontal line runs across the description then amount '0 15 0' is clear at right. Currency columns validated (15 shillings).
Name 'Franklyn.' clearly written. No monetary amount on this line.
Short surname 'Wynn.' read clearly; no associated amounts.
'Hart Hall' description clear. Amount '1 13 4' well aligned and digits are distinct; currency fields validate.
Reads 'Londen Hall' (older spelling of 'London'). Amount '0 10 0' clearly visible and aligned with the shillings/pence columns.
Appears to read 'Sadler.' (could be 'Sabler'/'Sadler'); letters slightly faint but intelligible. No amount present.
Surname 'Molineaux' (or variant) reasonably clear in cursive. No monetary columns populated.

Line contains two names/notes joined: 'Tintinhull' followed by a second scribble that reads like 'Japor' and then '/ Hopkins' beneath or adjacent. Hand is cramped and possibly two sub-entries on one line; no monetary figures. Transcribed literally as seen; reading uncertain.
Clear entry 'Clifton ferry.' No amount in the right-hand columns.
Reads as 'Boulington' though middle consonants are indistinct; could be 'Bovlington'/'Bonington'. No monetary values visible.
Final line in left column reads 'Brew-house' or 'Brewhouse' clearly. No amounts present.

Bottom-right aggregate total line. Amount '36 7 9' is clearly written on the lower-right corner; used these well-aligned digits to confirm column positions. Description area is a simple rule/blank on the page.
